steps.title: Identify the example phrase
steps.content: The phrase after the blank gives an example: such as the scent of roses when roses appeared in a scene. That example is additional information after the complete idea about odors being emitted at points in a movie, so it needs to be set off with punctuation.

steps.title: Choose the right boundary
steps.content: Choice A leaves the example unpunctuated. Choice B uses a semicolon before a phrase that is not an independent clause. Choice C turns the example into a sentence fragment. A comma before such as correctly introduces the nonessential example. The answer is D.
